verbatim: Add bounded relevance to late planned-fusion corpus reservations.
action: Re-verify the calibrated bound on late planned-fusion corpus reservations: `docker compose exec backend pytest tests/test_corpus_floor_calibration.py -q` (exit 0) and grep-confirm both planned_fusion reservation sites (planned_fusion.py:422-448, 1079-1118) route through the shared reservation_policy gate with no ungated path.

Terminology: canonical name is 'corpus reservation (reservation_policy gate)' — the bare word 'reservation' spans four mechanisms (lane reservation, corpus floor x3, finalist reservation, shelf-role seats); qualify it.
required_receipt: pytest tests/test_corpus_floor_calibration.py exit 0 in the backend container + grep output showing planned_fusion.py:422/1079 call the reservation_policy bound.

verbatim: Apply the same relevance gate before an already-selected corpus candidate is protected as a reservation. (live 2026-07-13: cross-corpus packet seats evidence, reasons in diagnostics)

required_receipt: SSE probe transcript showing cross-corpus packet seats evidence with per-candidate gate reasons in corpus_reservation_details; non-zero-exit assertion wrapper.

verbatim: Trace ranking_policy corpus-floor eligibility on calibrated packet scores rather than only normalized MMR relevance. (live 2026-07-13: cross-corpus packet seats evidence, reasons in diagnostics)
action: Re-verify receipt 'live 2026-07-13': confirm corpus_floor eligibility in ranking_policy traces calibrated packet scores — grep corpus_floor.reservation_bound at ranking_policy.py:1699 and re-run the cross-corpus probe asserting the bound (not raw normalized MMR) appears in selection.corpus_floor meta.

why: floor eligibility judged on calibrated scores keeps seats evidence-backed, not artifact-of-normalization
feasibility_note: registry_librarian: ranking_policy floor is 'calibrated via reservation_policy; eligibility trace, skip reasons; meta key corpus_floor at 1905'. Terminology: this is the retriever-side corpus_floor — the SAME diagnostics key name is also emitted by final_selector (different mechanism); do not conflate when auditing traces.
required_receipt: Probe diagnostics showing selection.corpus_floor.reservation_bound populated on a cross-corpus query + green test_corpus_floor_calibration.py.

verbatim: Consolidate or explicitly order the planned_fusion and ranking_policy corpus-floor decisions so one path cannot undo the other's rejection. (live 2026-07-13)
action: Re-verify the planned_fusion→ranking_policy ordering via tests/test_corpus_floor_calibration.py, THEN open a residual: final_selector.reserve_corpora (final_selector.py:312-362) still executes unconditionally at :572 with its own uncalibrated _strong_score_floor (0.80 ratio && 0.35 abs) even with SHELF_RESERVE_ENABLED — a third corpus-floor path outside the consolidation. Fold it through reservation_policy (or document explicit ordering) during S8 shelf_reserve wiring.
who: backend retriever; owner only for choosing consolidate-vs-ordering on the third path
why: one seat path silently undoing another's rejection reintroduces forced seats the calibration removed
feasibility_note: registry_librarian CONFIRMED failure mode: corpus_floor is 'one idea, three implementations, two bounds'; the ledger's claim that reserve_corpora is superseded by shelf_reserve is WRONG in code (reserve_shelf_roles seats only bridge/counterbalance roles, no per-corpus coverage; final_selector.py:572+576 run both passes back-to-back). The [x] is honest for the two named paths only. Also FAILURE MODE (a): two diagnostics keys both named 'corpus_floor' (ranking_policy selection.corpus_floor vs final_selector meta.corpus_floor) report different mechanisms.
required_receipt: Green calibration test for the two-path ordering + a commit (S8) gating or explicitly ordering final_selector.reserve_corpora through reservation_policy, with an asserting test that a rejection by one path is never reversed by another; diagnostics keys disambiguated.

verbatim: Remove or justify the unconditional +0.10 reserve bonus. (live 2026-07-13)
action: Re-verify receipt 'live 2026-07-13': grep ranking_policy/planned_fusion/final_selector for any unconditional reserve bonus (+0.10 or successor constant) — expect zero hits — and confirm reservation_policy's contract 'Neither path may inflate a score to protect a seat' (reservation_policy.py:19-24) is asserted in test_corpus_floor_calibration.py.
why: score inflation to protect seats corrupts calibrated packet scores downstream (rerank, answerability)
feasibility_note: registry evidence quotes the reservation_policy docstring adopting no-inflation as policy; the bonus removal is the documented 'remove' branch of remove-or-justify. Canonical term: corpus reservation bound, not 'reserve bonus'.
required_receipt: grep -rn '0.10' backend/services/retriever/{ranking_policy,planned_fusion}.py + final_selector.py showing no seat-protection bonus; calibration test green.

verbatim: Require diagnostics to distinguish naturally selected corpus evidence from quota-reserved evidence. (live 2026-07-13)

why: distinguishing quota seats from earned seats is what makes every later floor calibration auditable
feasibility_note: Diagnostics symbols verified in registry. One caveat carried from L412: the name 'corpus_floor' is emitted by two different mechanisms (ranking_policy and final_selector meta) — an auditor reading only the key name can attribute a quota seat to the wrong mechanism; disambiguation belongs to the S8 residual.
required_receipt: Probe diagnostics JSON showing selected_by='corpus_floor' present only on reserved items; run_heldout_eval consumption unchanged.

verbatim: Add a test where all selected corpora genuinely contribute. (tests/test_corpus_floor_calibration.py, green in container)

verbatim: No sub-threshold corpus receives a forced final seat. (live 2026-07-13: q046-class cross-corpus answers with evidence from both corpora)

verbatim: Relevant cross-corpus questions retain evidence from each necessary corpus.

why: proves cross-corpus questions keep every necessary corpus's evidence — the whole point of the floor

Eval substrate exists (P1.1 baseline [x] per S10 prerequisites); S8's shelf_reserve A/B on the held-out set is the natural, already-scheduled slot. 'Necessary corpus' needs a per-row expectation label in the eval set — small labeling task, not a blocker.
required_receipt: run_heldout_eval.py exit 0 with per-row cross-corpus coverage assertions green on the held-out 56 (S8 A/B run), report artifact retained.

verbatim: corpus_floor.skipped reports why a selected shelf was omitted. (live 2026-07-13)
action: Re-verify receipt 'live 2026-07-13': trigger a cross-corpus query where one selected corpus is omitted and assert corpus_floor_meta.skipped (ranking_policy.py:1801) names the omitted corpus with a reason.
why: an explained omission is the difference between a calibrated skip and a silent retrieval bug
feasibility_note: TERMINOLOGY FLAG: this acceptance line says 'shelf' where the code and mechanism mean CORPUS (registry_librarian collision note: P0.3/P1.4 use shelf=corpus; P1.5 shelf_engine uses shelf=query-relative role bucket, roles never persisted). Canonical reading: corpus_floor.skipped reports why a selected CORPUS was omitted. Keep 'shelf' out of P0.3 wording to avoid seeding the collision further.

verbatim: Chat-facing negative control currently fails closed for the tungsten query.
action: Re-check the fact: re-run the tungsten negative-control probe through the authed /api/chat SSE path and assert answerable=false with a fail-closed refusal (this row is the fact-claim twin of acceptance L450).
why: negative controls failing closed is the non-negotiable invariant the whole P0.4 section serves
feasibility_note: Consistent with the 2026-07-13 live tungsten receipt on L450. Terminology (registry_retrieval): 'answerability' = the chat-side gate decision; the retriever's _evaluate_sufficiency is its upstream REPAIR signal, not a second answerability — keep the retriever gate strict, tune only the chat gate.
required_receipt: SSE probe transcript: tungsten query returns answerable=false, refusal text, non-zero-exit assertion wrapper green.

verbatim: Rename or clearly separate lane coverage from answerability in every diagnostic contract. (live 2026-07-13)
action: Re-verify receipt 'live 2026-07-13': confirm diagnostic contracts expose lane coverage and answerability as distinct fields (selection.lane_coverage at retriever/__init__.py:3135 vs the chat gate verdict), and log the residual: production computes coverage via the inline chat_orchestrator._evidence_lane_coverage (:4240) while the unit-tested evidence_allocation.lane_coverage (:166) is dead code — delegate like select_lane_support already is.
who: backend chat orchestrator + retriever; junior-agent for the delegation cleanup
why: conflating recall coverage with answerability was the root of dishonest refusals; the split must stay visible
feasibility_note: Separation is live-receipted. TERMINOLOGY FLAG (registry_retrieval, OVERLAPPING): 'lane coverage' is TWO real concepts — recall_lane_coverage (QueryPlan recall lanes, sense 1) and evidence_lane_coverage (EvidencePlan comparison sides, sense 2); every diagnostic contract must say which. The duplicate-implementation drift (tests certify dead code) is a genuine open hygiene item, cheap to fix in S0-class cleanup.
required_receipt: Probe showing both fields distinctly named in diagnostics + (residual) a commit delegating chat coverage to evidence_allocation.lane_coverage with existing tests now covering production.

verbatim: Calibrate evidence sufficiency by query/answer shape, not one universal threshold. (live 2026-07-13)
action: Re-verify receipt 'live 2026-07-13': inspect services/answerability_tuning.py knobs for shape-keyed thresholds and probe two query shapes (single-fact vs relationship) confirming different sufficiency profiles apply (RELATIONSHIP_GATE lenient default per commit 4e47cdb).
who: backend retriever/chat gates; junior-agent for the probe
why: one universal threshold either refuses good relationship answers or admits junk single-fact ones
feasibility_note: Live receipt + shared-knob architecture confirmed in registry. Residual generic-atom calibration is explicitly re-homed to the production-redesign Phase 2 (per L452's receipt), i.e. deliberately out of P0.4 scope — do not re-open here.
required_receipt: Two-shape probe transcript showing shape-dependent sufficiency verdicts + answerability_tuning.py knob diff vs one-threshold baseline.

verbatim: Require answer presence, evidence strength, obligation coverage, and contradiction checks before answerable=true. [PARTIAL @f2fb6e2 — undecomposed queries now judged by the strict evidence-atom gate instead of synthetic-lane coverage; contradiction checks not yet implemented]
action: Owner decision then build: decide whether the contradiction check stays in P0.4 scope or is re-scoped (S0 row explicitly says 'close the P0.4 contradiction-check partial or re-scope it'). If build: design the contradiction signal (evidence-atom level), implement in the chat gate path, and add contradiction assertions to test_answerability_honesty — the ledger verified ZERO contradiction code and ZERO contradiction test assertions exist (2026-07-13).
who: owner (scope decision) ; junior-agent (design + implementation + asserting test)
where: backend/services/chat_orchestrator.py (gate), backend/services/answerability_tuning.py, backend/tests/test_answerability_honesty*.py; ledger box at RAPTOR checklist P0.4
why: answerable=true over mutually contradicting evidence is a silent-wrongness class no other gate catches
feasibility_note: Honestly tagged PARTIAL @f2fb6e2; the strict evidence-atom gate for undecomposed queries is real (canonical term: evidence_atom, registry SAME). The remaining piece was deliberately NOT built in S0 pending its own design — flipping the box requires either the check + test or a recorded re-scope (Standing Rule 3 forbids leaving the tag to drift). Risk if built naively: a cheap NLI/heuristic contradiction check adds latency on the hot path — design must state budget.
required_receipt: Either (a) commit + test_answerability_honesty contradiction assertions green in container and box flipped, or (b) ledger edit re-scoping the sub-item with owner sign-off and a named new home.

verbatim: Surface a precise refusal reason when sources cover a nearby but different concept. (live 2026-07-13: refusal names real concepts + nearest documents)
action: Re-verify receipt 'live 2026-07-13': probe a near-miss query (sources cover an adjacent concept) and assert the refusal names the real covered concepts plus nearest documents rather than a generic 'cannot answer'.

feasibility_note: Live receipt is specific (refusal names real concepts + nearest documents) and matches the honest-answerability goal; no registry collision on these terms.
required_receipt: Probe transcript of a near-miss query whose refusal lists covered concepts + nearest doc labels.

verbatim: Negative controls across all three tiers return answerable=false. (live 2026-07-13: tungsten fails closed on probe)

where: RetrievalTier dispatch (backend/services/retriever/ranking_policy.py:146,497,937); chat gate; probe harness
why: the non-negotiable invariant requires every retrieval behavior proven per-tier, and fail-closed is the invariant most prone to tier-specific regressions
feasibility_note: Almost certainly true given the shared gate sits above tier dispatch, but the receipt ('tungsten fails closed on probe') is one probe, not three — under the ledger's own contract ('all three tiers') the [x] is unproven as written. Cheap to close: one scripted 3-tier run. This is also a standing regression-matrix row (final gate S14), hence CONT.
required_receipt: Scripted probe output: 3 tiers x >=2 negative controls, all answerable=false, exit 0; archived for the S14 regression matrix.

verbatim: Strong answers are not rejected solely because calibrated score ranges differ by query type. (live 2026-07-13: q046 answers; residual generic-atom calibration tracked in redesign Phase 2)

feasibility_note: Receipt is live and names its residual explicitly; the generic-atom follow-up is SUPERSEDED into the redesign program (cross-encoder as sole scoring authority), which is the correct home — re-doing it inside P0.4 would violate one-migration-per-seam thinking.
required_receipt: q046 probe answering (answerable=true) + grep of RAG_PRODUCTION_REDESIGN.md Phase 2 showing the generic-atom calibration line item.

verbatim: Lane coverage and answerability are separately visible in UI/MCP output. (live 2026-07-13)
action: Re-verify receipt 'live 2026-07-13': call the MCP endpoint and the UI trace for one query, asserting lane coverage and answerability appear as SEPARATE named fields in both outputs.

where: MCP output contract (polymath-mcp container); UI live-trace tabs (frontend, live-trace-tabs.spec.ts); backend/services/retriever/__init__.py:3135

feasibility_note: Live receipt exists. Carry the L437 terminology flag into the surface contract: field names should distinguish recall_lane_coverage vs evidence_lane_coverage (two senses, registry OVERLAPPING) — a UI/MCP field just called 'lane_coverage' re-creates the ambiguity this box was opened to kill.
required_receipt: MCP response JSON + UI trace screenshot/spec assertion showing both fields separately named.

section: P0.5 Complete Chunk And Metadata Hygiene
verbatim: Filter separator-only candidates before reranking.

section: P0.5 Complete Chunk And Metadata Hygiene
verbatim: Gate broad one-word hand-authored facet aliases by specificity.
action: Re-verify via code + test: _specific_content_alias (normalizer.py:632-646) rejects one-word hand-authored content-facet aliases at stamping, and query-side SINGLE_TOKEN_ALIAS_DETECTION_KEYS (query_semantics.py:490-497) gates detection; run the facet normalizer tests green.

feasibility_note: Both gates verified in-repo. IMPORTANT SCOPE LIMIT (registry_facet): the gate blocks ONE-WORD aliases only — multiword broad aliases ('self determination', 'value alignment') still pass, so agency_preservation continues stamping NEW ingests; and 89,088/38,492 historical stamps remain. That remainder is the S7 owner decision (DF-based rights removal), not a defect in this box as written. Note broad-alias suppression now exists in TWO mechanisms (ingest-side + query-side); S7's DF rule would be a third — the plan must name all three or one gets rebuilt as another.
required_receipt: pytest green on normalizer alias-gate cases + grep of both gate symbols.

section: P0.5 Complete Chunk And Metadata Hygiene

section: P0.5 Complete Chunk And Metadata Hygiene
verbatim: Backfill missing doc_name, filename, and source identity on legacy payloads.
action: Build the legacy-payload backfill: deterministic script mapping Mongo documents → parent_chunks/Qdrant payloads to fill missing doc_name, filename, and source identity; capture-side fields ride S2 (T-HOOK-3 front-matter capture), the payload REWRITE batches with the S5 rematerialization so payloads rewrite once (Standing Rule 4).
where: Mongo documents/parent_chunks; per-corpus Qdrant payloads; backend/services/retriever/assembly.py:128 (current doc_id fallback is the symptom); new backend/scripts backfill
why: final sources without a document label are uncitable — invariant requires source provenance on all final evidence
feasibility_note: Deterministic and cheap (no LLM): doc identity already exists at the documents level; verified data state shows the gap is on legacy payloads, not missing source data. Sequencing per Standing Rule 4: do NOT run a standalone payload-rewrite pass — batch with S5's reindex (the same rule PLAN_CRITIQUE:86 applies to S7 cleanup).
required_receipt: Backfill script run log + post-run probe: 0 parent payloads missing doc_name/filename/source identity per corpus (count query), assembly label fallback rate = 0 on a live probe.

section: P0.5 Complete Chunk And Metadata Hygiene
verbatim: Move remaining curated content aliases from Python into a versioned lexicon dataset.
action: Migrate curated content aliases out of Python into the S5 versioned alias registry: absorb the THREE existing curated stores — CONCEPT_ALIASES (query_semantics.py:527), entity_aliases.json (neo4j_writer.py:43), canonical_families.json (neo4j_writer.py:46) — into the corpus_lexicon materialization seam (materialize_entries, corpus_lexicon.py:1593) with epoch invalidation; delete the Python constants behind a compatibility shim.
who: junior-agent (migration) ; owner only if a curated alias conflicts with machine-mined lexicon entries
why: hardcoded topic rules in Python violate the non-negotiable invariant and can't be versioned, audited, or per-corpus scoped
feasibility_note: Explicitly scheduled in S5 (PLAN_CRITIQUE:84 'fold P0.5 curated-aliases-out-of-Python into the same registry'). TERMINOLOGY FLAG (registry_alias): 'curated aliases' collides — retrieval-side CONCEPT_ALIASES vs graph-side canonical_families use the identical phrase for different mechanisms in different keyspaces (canonical_key/normalize_identity vs canonicalize_entity_name/NFKD); the migration must state which stores it absorbs. FAILURE MODE (b) risk: building the registry as a NEW store makes it the 4th/5th parallel alias system — checklist:817 forbids a parallel concept store; absorb into corpus_lexicon.
required_receipt: Commit removing the Python constants + registry dataset with schema_version + asserting test that query expansion resolves a formerly-curated alias through the registry (and grep: zero curated alias literals left in query_semantics/neo4j_writer).

section: P0.5 Complete Chunk And Metadata Hygiene
verbatim: Add artifact diagnostics for facet source, confidence, and evidence.
action: Build facet artifact diagnostics exposing, per stamped facet: provenance (surface BOTH source_level AND content_facet_source under those exact names), confidence, and the evidence text/alias that triggered the stamp; co-schedule the payload change with the S7 facet cleanup so facet payloads rewrite once.
why: S7's DF-based rights decision and the P0.5 acceptance (no incidental stamping) are only auditable if each stamp carries its source and evidence
feasibility_note: TERMINOLOGY FLAG (registry_facet source_level row): 'facet source' is 5-way overloaded — source_level (facet provenance), content_facet_source (which text produced it), semantic_facets.source='ingestion', coverage-facet source (ingest_facet_profile|vector_facet_probe), and document source_tier/source_refs. This box MUST specify which; the registry's recommendation (surface source_level + content_facet_source verbatim) is adopted in the action. Fields largely exist at write time — this is exposure + payload projection, not new computation.
required_receipt: Probe of a stamped parent showing facet diagnostics {source_level, content_facet_source, confidence, evidence} + asserting test on the diagnostic contract.

section: P0.5 Complete Chunk And Metadata Hygiene
verbatim: No separator/navigation artifact can enter a final evidence packet.
action: Close the acceptance end-to-end: (1) add an asserting test that no chunk in the FINAL evidence packet (all three tiers) passes is_separator_only_text or NOISY_KINDS; (2) one-time audit + purge of legacy separator/navigation children still in stores (pre-L459 ingests), batched with the S5 reindex; (3) keep the pre-rerank filter (L460) as defense in depth.
who: junior-agent (test + audited purge script, snapshot+restore first)
where: backend/services/retriever/ranking_policy.py:1717-1855 + reservation_policy + final_selector (three modules co-own finalization); NOISY_KINDS junk filter; backend/services/text_quality.py; Qdrant/Mongo child stores
why: candidate-stage filtering is not a packet guarantee — the acceptance is about what can reach the user
feasibility_note: registry_leftover pins the seam: 'no final_evidence symbol; three modules co-own finalization... nearest seam is the NOISY_KINDS junk filter'. Ingest drop (L459) and pre-rerank filter (L460) are live, so the residual exposure is legacy stored children and any packet path that bypasses the fused-candidate filter — hence the final-packet assertion, not another mid-pipeline filter. Purge is destructive: non-destructive snapshot+restore verification required per assert-before-commit.
required_receipt: 3-tier asserting test green (no separator/NOISY_KINDS chunk in final packets) + store audit query returning 0 separator-only children post-purge.

section: P0.5 Complete Chunk And Metadata Hygiene
verbatim: No broad incidental word stamps unrelated domain facets.
action: Blocked pending S7: adopt the DF-threshold rule (any facet with per-corpus parent-DF above threshold loses retrieval/expansion rights, evidence retained — owner decision), apply to the broad-alias family (agency_preservation 89,088; emotional_patterns 38,492), extend the specificity gate to multiword broad aliases, THEN verify this acceptance with a probe that incidental word use no longer stamps or ranks by unrelated domain facets.
who: owner (S7 threshold/rights decision) ; junior-agent (rule implementation + cleanup riding S5 reindex)
where: backend/services/facets/normalizer.py:167-357 (_CONTENT_FACET_ALIASES),632; backend/services/retriever/query_semantics.py:490-497; facet payloads (cleanup batched with S5 per PLAN_CRITIQUE:86); proposed HIGH_DF_FACET_FAMILY
why: a facet stamped on 71-100% of every corpus is DF-worthless and can only add noise to facet-aware ranking
feasibility_note: Blocked on the S7 owner decision (PLAN_CRITIQUE:86 marks it OWNER DECISION; pending decision 'blocks calling P0.5 acceptance honest'). Registry evidence: agency_preservation's multiword aliases still pass the one-word gate, so this is NOT legacy-only cleanup — new ingests keep stamping until the rule lands. One persisted facet-DF stat should feed both S7 and P2.1 (audit_corpus:177-236 is the nearest seam) — three parallel DF stats is the named failure mode.
required_receipt: Owner-recorded S7 decision + commit applying the DF rule + probe: incidental-word fixture text stamps zero unrelated domain facets and broad-family facets exert zero retrieval/expansion influence (diagnostics show rights removed).

section: P0.5 Complete Chunk And Metadata Hygiene
verbatim: Every final source has a usable document label and source boundary.

who: junior-agent (probe + assertion) ; depends on L463 backfill

feasibility_note: Purely downstream of the L463 backfill plus existing boundary metadata (heading_path present 86-100% per PLAN_CRITIQUE data table; the 14% v2 gap and OCR-corrupt TEXT quality are S3-governed, so 'usable' should be defined as label-present + boundary-present, with heading TEXT quality explicitly deferred to the S3 repair rule to avoid scope-coupling).
